title: cycas siamensis subsp. balansae
intro: Cycas siamensis subsp. balansae, also known as the Siamese or Balansa cycad, is a rare and endangered species of cycad, a type of ancient plant that is often referred to as a "living fossil." Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine in Southeast Asia for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its seeds, leaves, and roots. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as the ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of extracts, powders, or infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and risks.
